name: Harvest
description_short: Harvest is time tracking and invoicing software for professional services firms, not a consumer finance product. It helps teams track billable hours, generate invoices, and analyze project profitability.
description_long: Harvest is a workforce management and invoicing platform founded in 2006 that serves professional services teams including consulting firms, design agencies, law firms, and software development companies. The platform is primarily a B2B software-as-a-service tool designed to help teams optimize their operations, not a consumer finance lender or credit product. Harvest's core offering includes time tracking across desktop, mobile, and web platforms, allowing employees to log billable hours and track project time in real-time. The platform automates invoice generation from tracked time, integrates with accounting software like Xero and QuickBooks, and enables online payment collection through Stripe and PayPal integrations. Beyond invoicing, Harvest provides reporting and analytics features that give business leaders visibility into project profitability, team capacity utilization, budget monitoring, and cost tracking across clients and projects. The platform distinguishes itself through simplicity and ease of adoption—evidenced by 70,000+ company users and multi-year customer retention—as well as deep integrations with tools like Asana, Slack, and accounting platforms that professional teams already use. Honest assessment: Harvest is fundamentally miscategorized as a payday alternative. It is not a consumer finance product, lending service, or credit product of any kind. It is enterprise time tracking and billing software that helps businesses optimize profitability. The platform serves B2B professional services firms with 50+ employees down to smaller teams, but provides no consumer lending, credit access, or financial relief services. Categorizing this as a payday alternative is factually incorrect and misleading to consumers seeking actual financial products.
services: Time tracking via desktop app, mobile app, and web browser with start/stop timers
Automated invoice generation from tracked hours with client payment links
Online payment processing integration with Stripe and PayPal
Project budget setting and real-time budget monitoring with alerts
Team capacity and utilization tracking to balance workloads and avoid burnout
Expense tracking and receipt uploads for billable and non-billable costs
Profitability reporting across projects, clients, and teams with margin analysis
Accounting software integration with Xero and QuickBooks for seamless billing workflows
Third-party app integrations with Asana, Slack, and other productivity tools
Project-level and team-level detailed reporting with drill-down analytics
Automated invoice reminders and follow-up for faster payment collection
Harvest Forecast feature for team scheduling and capacity planning
pros: Multi-platform time tracking (desktop Mac/PC, mobile iOS/Android, web browser) with push reminders to encourage compliance
Automated invoice generation in 2 clicks from tracked time, reducing manual billing work
Built-in budget monitoring with hourly or fixed-fee project budgets and instant alerts when budgets are reached
Integrated online payment processing via Stripe and PayPal directly from invoices for faster payment collection
Comprehensive reporting on profitability, team capacity, utilization, and project-level margins
Integrations with Xero, QuickBooks, Asana, and Slack to fit existing workflows
Free 30-day trial with no credit card required, allowing teams to test before committing
cons: Not a consumer finance or lending product—entirely inapplicable to personal loan, credit, or emergency cash needs
Pricing not displayed on website; requires sign-up or contact for cost information, which may be prohibitive for small solo practitioners
Designed for professional services teams and may be overkill for solo freelancers or very small businesses with minimal invoicing needs
Dependent on team adoption and consistent time tracking discipline; poor data quality if users don't log time reliably
Miscategorization as a payday alternative creates false expectations for consumers seeking actual financial products or lending services
best_for: Professional services firms (consulting, law, accounting, architecture) with 10+ employees needing to bill by the hour
Creative agencies and design teams that need visibility into project profitability and team capacity
Software development and IT firms managing multiple client projects with varying budget constraints
Management consulting practices and engineering firms that require detailed time tracking and margin analysis
diagnosis: Harvest is entirely miscategorized as a payday alternative. This is B2B time tracking and invoicing software for professional services firms, not a consumer financial product, lender, or credit service. Consumers seeking actual payday loans, personal loans, credit access, or financial assistance should not consider this company—it provides no relevant services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
